title: External Examples Schema
description: A schema for external examples in Specmatic

AnyValue.title: Any Value
AnyValue.description: Arbitrary JSON value accepted without additional structural constraints.

Metadata.title: Example Metadata
Metadata.description: Common metadata fields that can be attached to a full or partial external example.

transient.description: Whether this example should be treated as transient during execution.

delay-in-seconds.description: Delay to apply before serving this example, in whole seconds.

delay-in-milliseconds.description: Delay to apply before serving this example, in milliseconds.

http-stub-id.description: Identifier of the stub this example belongs to.

Example.title: Complete Example
Example.description: A complete external example containing a request, a response, and optional fixture hooks.

RequestResponseSchema.title: Request And Response Pair
RequestResponseSchema.description: Core request and response payload pair for a full external example.

http-request.description: HTTP request that should match this example.

http-response.description: HTTP response that should be returned for this example.

PartialExample.title: Partial Example
PartialExample.description: A partial external example used for partial matching, with optional shared metadata and fixtures.

partial.description: Partial request/response example used for partial matching.

FixtureHooks.title: Fixture Hooks
FixtureHooks.description: Collections of fixtures to run before or after an example executes.

before.description: Fixtures to execute before the example runs.

after.description: Fixtures to execute after the example runs.

Fixture.title: Fixture
Fixture.description: A fixture definition that can be attached to before or after hooks.

HttpFixture.title: HTTP Fixture
HttpFixture.description: An HTTP fixture that invokes an endpoint before or after an example runs.

type.description: Fixture type discriminator. Currently only http is supported.

wait.description: Delay to wait before executing the fixture.

timeout.description: Maximum time to wait for the fixture call to complete.

retry.description: Retry policy to apply when the fixture call does not succeed immediately.

executeFor.description: Controls which scenario types should execute this fixture.

http-request.description: HTTP request to send when executing the fixture.

http-response.description: Expected HTTP response returned by the fixture call.

Retry.title: Retry Policy
Retry.description: Retry policy applied when a fixture call does not succeed immediately.

max-attempts.description: Maximum number of retry attempts for the fixture call.

FixtureExecuteFor.title: Fixture Scenario Selector
FixtureExecuteFor.description: Selector that limits which scenario types should trigger a fixture.

scenarios.description: Run this fixture for all, positive, or negative scenarios.

HTTPFixtureRequest.title: HTTP Fixture Request
HTTPFixtureRequest.description: Request shape used when invoking an HTTP fixture.

baseUrl.description: Base URL of the fixture endpoint.

method.description: HTTP method to use for the fixture call.

path.description: Path to invoke on the fixture endpoint.

headers.description: Headers to send with the fixture request.

body.description: Request body to send with the fixture call.

HTTPFixtureResponse.title: HTTP Fixture Response
HTTPFixtureResponse.description: Expected response shape returned from an HTTP fixture call.

status.description: Expected HTTP status returned by the fixture call.

body.description: Expected response body returned by the fixture call.

PartialRequestResponseSchema.title: Partial Request And Response Pair
PartialRequestResponseSchema.description: Partial request and response payload pair used inside a partial external example.

RequestSchema.title: Example Request
RequestSchema.description: HTTP request fields that Specmatic should match for an example.

path.description: HTTP path that should match the incoming request.

method.description: HTTP method that should match the incoming request.

query.description: Query parameters expected on the incoming request.

headers.description: Headers expected on the incoming request.

body.description: Body expected on the incoming request.

form-fields.description: Form fields expected for form-encoded requests.

multipart-formdata.description: Multipart form-data parts expected on the incoming request.

bodyRegex.description: Regular expression the request body should satisfy.

ResponseSchema.title: Example Response
ResponseSchema.description: HTTP response fields that Specmatic should return for an example.

status.description: HTTP status to return for this example.

headers.description: Headers to return in the example response.

body.description: Body to return in the example response.

externalisedResponseCommand.description: External command used to compute the example response body.

MultiPartFormData.title: Multipart Form Data Part
MultiPartFormData.description: A multipart form-data part, represented either as inline content or as a file upload.

MultiPartContent.title: Multipart Inline Content
MultiPartContent.description: A multipart form-data field whose value is supplied inline as literal content.

name.description: Name of the multipart form-data field.

content.description: Literal content of the multipart form-data field.

contentType.description: Content type of the multipart form-data field.

MultiPartFile.title: Multipart File
MultiPartFile.description: A multipart form-data field whose value is supplied as a file reference.

contentEncoding.description: Content encoding of the multipart file part.

contentType.description: Content type of the multipart file part.

filename.description: Filename of the multipart file part.

name.description: Name of the multipart file field.
